data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project. Let me migrate from xUnit.net v2 to v3. Key changes needed:

1. **TestProject.csproj**: 
   - Replace `xunit` package with `xunit.v3` (or the appropriate v3 package)
   - Remove `xunit.runner.visualstudio` (v3 uses a different runner mechanism)
   - Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (v3 test projects are executables)
   - Change to `Exe` output type
   - Update target framework (v3 requires at least net6.0, but net8.0 is fine)
   
2. **global.json**: For xUnit v3, I can either keep using `dotnet test` with the v3 runner package, or switch to `dotnet run` since v3 projects are standalone executables. The approach depends on whether I want to maintain compatibility with the test runner infrastructure or embrace the executable model.

Now I'm working through the specific migration details for xUnit v3 — the package changes from `xunit` to `xunit.v3`, the test projects become executables rather than libraries, and I don't need `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` or `xunit.runner.visualstudio` anymore. The namespace stays as `Xunit` but some types have moved, and attributes like `BeforeAfterTestAttribute` have different method signatures in v3. for `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` or `xunit.runner.visualstudio`
- Project must be an Exe: `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>`
- Need to add an entry point (but xunit.v3 auto-generates one)
- `BeforeAfterTestAttribute` now has methods that take different parameters:
  - `Before(MethodInfo, IXunitTest)` or similar
  - Actually in v3, `BeforeAfterTestAttribute` methods are:
    - `Before(MethodInfo methodUnderTest, IXunitTest test)` 
    - `After(MethodInfo methodUnderTest, IXunitTest test)`

Let me check the actual v3 migration details more carefully. The key changes are replacing the old xunit packages with `xunit.v3`, removing the test runner packages, setting the project to an executable, and understanding that `BeforeAfterTestAttribute` now lives in the `Xunit.v3` namespace with `Before` and `After` methods that both return `ValueTask` and take a `MethodInfo` and `IXunitTest` parameter. I need to update the project file to swap out the test SDK and runner packages for xunit.v3, and make sure the output type is set to executable. The using statements need to change since BeforeAfterTestAttribute moved into the main Xunit namespace, and the method signatures now take both MethodInfo and IXunitTest parameters, potentially returning ValueTask instead of void. I should also check if there's any VSTest configuration in global.json that needs adjusting. Looking at the xUnit v3 API, `BeforeAfterTestAttribute` uses `ValueTask` methods that take `MethodInfo` and `IXunitTest` parameters, with the namespace moved to `Xunit` rather than `Xunit.Sdk`. I should update the attribute implementation to match this signature and then test the build to verify everything works correctly.

For the global.json configuration, I'm realizing the `test.runner` setting is likely for specifying `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` as the test runner in xUnit v3, which has replaced the older VSTest approach. I should update that configuration accordingly rather than removing it entirely. Since xUnit v3 uses Microsoft.Testing.Platform natively, I should update the `global.json` to either remove the test runner config or set it to use that platform instead of VSTest. Let me just make the changes and attempt a build, starting with the latest available xunit.v3 package version from NuGet.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
Contents of https://www.nuget.org/packages/xunit.v3:
*   [README](#readme-body-tab)
*   [Frameworks](#supportedframeworks-body-tab)
*   [Dependencies](#dependencies-body-tab)
*   [Used By](#usedby-body-tab)
*   [Versions](#versions-body-tab)
*   [Release Notes](#releasenotes-body-tab)

## xUnit.net v3

xUnit.net is a developer testing framework, built to support Test Driven Development, with a design goal of extreme simplicity and alignment with framework features. Supports .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later and .NET 8 or later.

Installing this package installs xunit.v3.mtp-v1.

## About xUnit.net

xUnit.net is a free, open source, community-focused unit testing tool for C#, F#, and Visual Basic. xUnit.net v3 supports .NET 8.0 or later, and .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later.

xUnit.net works with the [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) command line tools, [Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/), [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/), [NCrunch](https://www.ncrunch.net/), and any development environment compatible with [Microsoft Testing Platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro) or [VSTest](https://github.com/microsoft/vstest).

xUnit.net is part of the [.NET Foundation](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/) and operates under their [code of conduct](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct). It is licensed under [Apache 2](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) (an OSI approved license). The project is [governed](https://xunit.net/governance) by a Project Lead.

For project documentation, please visit the [xUnit.net project home](https://xunit.net/).

*   _New to xUnit.net? Get started with the [.NET SDK](https://xunit.net/docs/getting-started/v3/getting-started)._
*   _Need some help building the source? See [BUILDING.md](https://github.com/xunit/xunit/tree/main/BUILDING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the project? See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/xunit/.github/tree/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the assertion library? See the [suggested contribution workflow](https://github.com/xunit/assert.xunit/tree/main/README.md#suggested-contribution-workflow) in the assertion library project, as it is slightly more complex due to code being spread across two GitHub repositories._

[![Powered by NDepend](https://raw.github.com/xunit/media/main/powered-by-ndepend-transparent.png)](http://www.ndepend.com/)
